title: rubus contritidens
intro: Rubus contritidens, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of edible fruit-bearing shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The medicinal properties of Rubus contritidens are attributed to its high content of anthocyanins and other polyphenols, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of consuming Rubus contritidens include reducing the risk of heart disease, improving cognitive function, and providing relief from digestive issues. Rubus contritidens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other culinary applications.
